title: geum rivale subsp. subalpinum
intro: Geum rivale subsp. subalpinum, also known as the Alpine Avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family, Rosaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Geum rivale subsp. subalpinum include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive problems, and even exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various ailments.
